Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Monuses and Heaps 35 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 152 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. My first patch to the Linux kernel 176 upvotes, 32 comments
  4. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 31 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 75 upvotes, 14 comments
  6. A review of dice that came with the white castle 77 upvotes, 17 comments
  7. 25 Years of Eggs 162 upvotes, 49 comments
  8. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 254 upvotes, 86 comments
  9. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 331 upvotes, 118 comments
  10. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 192 upvotes, 67 comments
  11. HopTab – Open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 86 upvotes, 26 comments
  12. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 25 upvotes, 5 comments
  13. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 70 upvotes, 25 comments
  14. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 556 upvotes, 323 comments
  15. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 410 upvotes, 241 comments
  16. Chest Fridge (2009) 164 upvotes, 88 comments
  17. A Coherent Vision for the Future of Version Control 30 upvotes, 11 comments
  18. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 231 upvotes, 137 comments
  19. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 42 upvotes, 20 comments
  20. Brute-Forcing My Algorithmic Ignorance with an LLM in 7 Days 50 upvotes, 27 comments
  21. I hate: Programming Wayland applications 94 upvotes, 65 comments
  22. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 239 upvotes, 194 comments
  23. Why Lab Coats Turned White 30 upvotes, 19 comments
  24. iBook Clamshell 40 upvotes, 30 comments
  25. Windows native app development is a mess 116 upvotes, 111 comments
  26. Apple's intentional crippling of Mobile Safari 110 upvotes, 120 comments
  27. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 14 upvotes, 11 comments
  28. A case against currying 49 upvotes, 60 comments
  29. You Are Not Your Job 8 upvotes, 6 comments
  30. Ask HN: AI productivity gains – do you fire devs or build better products? 38 upvotes, 55 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
